description:
You are an MLOps engineer who has just finished running experiment “exp42” locally and now need to archive the resulting artifacts to a **simulated remote location** on the same machine.  
All actions must be performed from the Linux terminal, using any standard POSIX utilities available in a default Ubuntu-like environment.  
Your goal is to copy the artifacts and create a simple synchronization log that an automated test will later verify.

Initial system state  
1. Directory “/home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42” already exists and contains exactly two files.  
   • /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42/model.pkl – a small text-based placeholder for a serialized model.  
   • /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42/metrics.json – a JSON file with evaluation metrics.  
2. Directory “/home/user/remote_store” exists and is **empty**. Think of it as a mounted remote share.  
3. Directory “/home/user/mlops/logs” exists and is empty.

Task  
A. Synchronize the whole directory “/home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42” into  
   “/home/user/remote_store/exp42”.  
   • The destination directory must exist after the operation and must contain byte-for-byte identical copies of both “model.pkl” and “metrics.json”.  
   • No additional or extraneous files may be present in “/home/user/remote_store/exp42”.

B. Create a plain-text log file “/home/user/mlops/logs/exp42_sync.log” **with exactly four newline-terminated lines** (no leading/trailing spaces).  
   The required, fully deterministic contents are:

   Line 1: SYNC_START:2024-06-01T12:00:00Z  
   Line 2: SRC:/home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42  
   Line 3: DST:/home/user/remote_store/exp42  
   Line 4: STATUS:SUCCESS  

C. Verify that the log and the copied files are present by listing them to the console so the grader can capture the output.

Constraints & hints  
• Yourself: regular user “user”; do not use sudo.  
• You should be able to accomplish the entire task with **no more than three commands** (one to ensure the destination directory is present, one to copy/synchronize, one to create the log file – though the exact breakdown is up to you).  
• Do not hard-code absolute timestamps from your system; use the fixed timestamp shown above.  
• The automated test will check:  
  1. That “/home/user/remote_store/exp42/model.pkl” and “…/metrics.json” exist and are identical to the originals.  
  2. That “/home/user/mlops/logs/exp42_sync.log” exists with the four exact lines (including newline characters).  
  3. That no extra files were created inside “/home/user/remote_store/exp42”.  
  4. That you printed the file listings to the terminal.

Produce only the terminal commands and their outputs (exact echoes of ls or cat etc.) in your interaction; do not add explanations outside of normal shell output.
truth:
Pre-existing resources before the agent starts  
1. Path: /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42/model.pkl  
   Contents (single line, newline-terminated):  
   -----  
   DUMMY MODEL DATA  
   -----  
2. Path: /home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42/metrics.json  
   Contents (pretty-printed, newline-terminated):  
   {  
     "accuracy": 0.93,  
     "loss": 0.12  
   }  

Expected state after successful task completion  
1. Path: /home/user/remote_store/exp42/model.pkl  
   • File must exist and its contents must match **exactly** the contents of the source model.pkl.  
2. Path: /home/user/remote_store/exp42/metrics.json  
   • File must exist and its contents must match **exactly** the contents of the source metrics.json.  
3. Directory: /home/user/remote_store/exp42  
   • Must contain only the two files listed above and no other entries (apart from “.” and “..”).  
4. Path: /home/user/mlops/logs/exp42_sync.log  
   • File must exist with size 144 bytes (including newlines) and contain exactly these four lines:  
     SYNC_START:2024-06-01T12:00:00Z\n  
     SRC:/home/user/mlops/experiments/exp42\n  
     DST:/home/user/remote_store/exp42\n  
     STATUS:SUCCESS\n  

Grader checks  
• Uses sha256sum to compare source and destination files.  
• Verifies directory listing count == 2.  
• Verifies exp42_sync.log byte-for-byte equality with template.  
• Greps agent’s final console output for “exp42_sync.log” and “remote_store/exp42”.
